title: The End of Your Second Year
prose: Second year ends more quietly than it began — no fanfare, just the same exams, the same packing, the same long train ride south with your trunk taking up too much space under the seat.
Next year brings electives — actual choices about what you learn, for the first time since you arrived. You spend most of the ride home arguing with your dormmates about which ones are worth taking.

choices.label: Head home already turning the choices over.
